questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: It is the second largest producer of coal in the world. Q: India has vast mineral resources. R: It also has significant reserves of iron ore and bauxite. S: The mining sector contributes substantially to the economy.

questions.stem: Antonym of LACONIC:

questions.stem: Rearrange: P: The internet has revolutionised communication. Q: People can now connect instantly across the globe. R: Social media platforms enable sharing of information. S: However, digital divide remains a concern.

questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Vaccines have saved millions of lives worldwide. Q: They work by training the immune system to fight diseases. R: Herd immunity protects those who cannot be vaccinated. S: Vaccine development requires rigorous clinical trials.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: I am looking forward to meet you.
options: I am looking forward for meeting you.
I look forward to meet you.
I am looking forward to meeting you.
I am looking forward to meet you.
questions.solution: I am looking forward to meeting you.

He asked me to convey him my regards.
questions.solution: He asked me to convey to him my regards.

questions.subject: Mechanical Engineering
questions.stem: Cut-off ratio in diesel cycle is:
options: Ratio of volumes at heat addition start and end
questions.solution: Cut-off: V3/V2. Heat added at constant pressure.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For an ideal gas, internal energy depends only on:
questions.solution: U = f(T) only for ideal gas (Joule's law). Enthalpy h = u + pv = f(T) as well.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Centreless grinding is used for:
questions.solution: Centreless grinding: mass production of cylindrical parts.

questions.subject: Mechanical Engineering
questions.stem: Fatigue limit of non-ferrous metals:
options: Does not exist (use endurance strength at specified life)
questions.solution: Non-ferrous: no true fatigue limit. Use endurance strength.

questions.stem: For the same machine, the COP of a heat pump and that of a refrigerator are related by:
questions.solution: Heat rejected = heat absorbed + work, so COP_HP = COP_ref + 1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Minor losses:
options: KL·V²/(2g) in fittings, valves, bends, entrances. K_L = loss coefficient
questions.solution: Minor losses: sum as equivalent length. K_L values from tables. Sudden expansion: K_L = (1-A₁/A₂)².

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Gear force analysis — spur:
options: Tangential force F_t = T/r. Radial force F_r = F_t·tanφ. φ = pressure angle
questions.solution: Pressure angle: 20° (standard), 25° (stronger tooth). F_t drives, F_r separates.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The compression ratio is:
questions.solution: CR = V₁/V₂ = (V_s + V_c)/V_c. Gasoline CR ≈ 8-11. Diesel CR ≈ 14-22.

options: It equals the Carnot engine efficiency
questions.solution: COP_ref = Q_cold/W can be well above 1 because it moves more heat than the work supplied.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Between two fixed temperature reservoirs, the Carnot engine is special because it has:
options: The maximum possible efficiency, being fully reversible
The maximum work but lower efficiency than real engines
questions.solution: No engine between the same two temperatures can exceed Carnot efficiency, because Carnot is reversible.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The endurance (fatigue) limit of a material is the stress below which it:
options: Can endure virtually infinite load cycles
questions.solution: Below the endurance limit, ferrous materials survive an effectively unlimited number of cycles.

options: Adverse pressure gradient retards flow near wall

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Young's modulus (E):
options: Ratio of normal stress to normal strain. Same units as stress (GPa, MPa)
questions.solution: E = σ/ε. Steel: 200 GPa. Aluminum: 70 GPa. Copper: 110 GPa.

questions.solution: Venturi: constriction creates pressure drop. Q = Cd * A2 * sqrt(2*dP/rho).
